story.title: Alveraz_Maria_OH2770 COVID-19 Oral History

lines.line: >Stephanie Milne-Lane and Maria Alvarez introduce themselves. Alvarez provides background information on her family immigrating to the United States from Mexico.

metadata.title: Introductions
metadata.synopsis: Stephanie Milne-Lane and Maria Alvarez introduce themselves. Alvarez provides background information on her family immigrating to the United States from Mexico.

lines.line: >Alvarez explains how she first heard about Covid-19. She offers insight into how the pandemic was especially difficult due to her children's complex medical issues.

metadata.title: COVID-19
metadata.synopsis: Alvarez explains how she first heard about Covid-19. She offers insight into how the pandemic was especially difficult due to her children's complex medical issues.

lines.line: >Alvarez shares how she established her business, Contectate. She articulates the mission of the business and the circumstances that led her to start the business. She describes her biggest obstacles and greatest achievements since establishing her business.

metadata.title: Conectate

lines.line: >Alvarez shares how her community of Idaho Falls has evolved throughout the pandemic. She touches on mask mandates and vaccines. She also mentions in the substantial growth that the Idaho Falls community has experienced since the onset of the pandemic.

metadata.title: Pandemic in Idaho Falls
metadata.synopsis: Alvarez shares how her community of Idaho Falls has evolved throughout the pandemic. She touches on mask mandates and vaccines. She also mentions in the substantial growth that the Idaho Falls community has experienced since the onset of the pandemic.

lines.line: >Alvarez shares how attending church changes during the pandemic. She provides details on what it was like to start attending church again in person.

metadata.title: Church
metadata.synopsis: Alvarez shares how attending church changes during the pandemic. She provides details on what it was like to start attending church again in person.

lines.line: >Alvarez describes the hardship of educating her children when schools closed. She offers detail on how she advocated and supported her children during the pandemic.

metadata.title: Education

lines.line: >Alvarez explains new habits and interests she picked up during the pandemic, including resting, meditating, and healthy eating.

metadata.title: New Habits
metadata.synopsis: Alvarez explains new habits and interests she picked up during the pandemic, including resting, meditating, and healthy eating.

lines.line: Alvarez shares that she was hesitant of getting the Covid-19 vaccine because of past experiences. She explains how she and her family eventually got vaccinated. She also describes contracting the virus after she had been vaccinated. She details her symptoms and her recovery.

metadata.title: Vaccines
metadata.notes: Alvarez shares that she was hesitant of getting the Covid-19 vaccine because of past experiences. She explains how she and her family eventually got vaccinated. She also describes contracting the virus after she had been vaccinated. She details her symptoms and her recovery.

children.text: >Stephanie Milne-Lane and Maria Alvarez introduce themselves. Alvarez provides background information on her family immigrating to the United States from Mexico.
>Alvarez explains how she first heard about Covid-19. She offers insight into how the pandemic was especially difficult due to her children's complex medical issues.

>Alvarez shares how she established her business, Contectate. She articulates the mission of the business and the circumstances that led her to start the business. She describes her biggest obstacles and greatest achievements since establishing her business.

>Alvarez shares how her community of Idaho Falls has evolved throughout the pandemic. She touches on mask mandates and vaccines. She also mentions in the substantial growth that the Idaho Falls community has experienced since the onset of the pandemic.
>Alvarez shares how attending church changes during the pandemic. She provides details on what it was like to start attending church again in person.
>Alvarez describes the hardship of educating her children when schools closed. She offers detail on how she advocated and supported her children during the pandemic.
>Alvarez explains new habits and interests she picked up during the pandemic, including resting, meditating, and healthy eating.

Alvarez shares that she was hesitant of getting the Covid-19 vaccine because of past experiences. She explains how she and her family eventually got vaccinated. She also describes contracting the virus after she had been vaccinated. She details her symptoms and her recovery.

indexes.title: Index to Oral History Interview with Maria Alvarez

story.description: Maria Alvarez shares her experiences as a mother and business owner during the COVID-19 pandemic.
